evidence.detail: Admin authentication is conditional on an Option; None skips all token and role checks.
Initialize, membership, and write handlers directly mutate live Raft state.
Follower forwarding uses the membership leader URL and propagates the caller token.

suppression: With a provisioned anchor, require_admin verifies the WSF token and exact aog-admin role before every guarded operation. One-hop forwarding re-authenticates the propagated token on the leader and has an anti-loop header.

evidence.detail: OpenBao login and KV read errors fail startup closed.
All four trust fields are required, base64 decoded, and key constructors enforce material lengths.

suppression: Malformed/missing trust material fails startup. The bridge permits HTTP OpenBao URLs, but secure-production examples use HTTPS and the plaintext demo networks are explicitly classified as non-production; no new AOG-specific instance was promoted from this file.

evidence.detail: Missing anchor and OpenBao configuration is accepted as state=None; advertise defaults to HTTP.
Daemon starts Raft before selecting optional trust material; absent material leaves state None.
Wire and admin surfaces are always mounted, while authenticated CRUD is conditional.

suppression: Trust material supplied through OpenBao takes precedence and fails startup closed. The raw anchor path still authenticates admin/CRUD, though it uses the documented kernel sealer seam.
